# hasProperty(_:)

**Instance Method**

Returns a Boolean value indicating whether the JavaScript value has a defined property with the specified name.

## Declaration

```swift
func hasProperty(_ property: Any!) -> Bool
```

```swift
func hasProperty(_ property: String!) -> Bool
```

## Parameters

- **property**: The name of a property to query for in the JavaScript object value.

## Return Value

[doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true] if the JavaScript object has a defined property by that name; otherwise, [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/false].

## Working with Container Values

- **defineProperty(_:descriptor:)**: Defines a property on the JavaScript object value or modifies a property’s definition.
- **deleteProperty(_:)**: Deletes the named property from the JavaScript object value.
- **atIndex(_:)**: Returns the value at the specified numeric index in the JavaScript object value.
- **setValue(_:at:)**: Sets the value at the specified numeric index in the JavaScript object value.
- **forProperty(_:)**: Returns the value of the named property in the JavaScript object value.
- **setValue(_:forProperty:)**: Sets the value of the named property in the JavaScript object value.
- **JSValueProperty**: A type that identifies a property of a JavaScript value.
